# Transcrição: 15- Chimney Fire

- **Fonte:** 15- Chimney Fire
- **Duração:** 06:42
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:02]

Sitting at the dining room table, I'm staring at a white plate with 10 peas on it, 10 green peas, one for each year that I've been alive. This is what my mother does. She makes me eat the food that I hate the most in equivalent amounts to my years alive. She thinks that this is going to change my life, these 10 peas. And while I'm staring at these peas wondering how the hell I'm going to get rid of them, my brother Jeremy asks from the other side of the table who the vice president of the United States is. Ronald Reagan has just been shot in there. We're not sure if he's going to make it and Jeremy's wondering what's going to happen. And I know the answer to this question. So I shouted out, Walter Mondale. I think it's Walter Mondale because I just went through a fourth grade election. And in that election, the person who gets the most votes becomes president and the person who gets second most votes becomes vice president. So I just assume it's Walter Mondale. But from the other side of the table, the head of the table, my stepfather Neil, he doesn't say a word, he just makes this sound, this tiny verbal explosion. It is his way of letting me know that I am wrong and he's going to humiliate me while proving that I am wrong. This is like his favorite thing to do his past time and he is really good at it because he's an adult and he has these terrible things called facts. And I'm a ten-year-old boy who thinks he knows everything. And I'm like that awful version of a ten-year-old boy who only reads books to find things that he can tell other people who don't know them. So like I am constantly charging into battles against Neil and I am losing every one of them. Like the time I thought Washington State and Washington DC were the same place. Or the time I thought Babe Ruth's head was one of the heads on Mount Rushmore. Or the knock-down drag-out fight over my certainty that a two-headed camel was a fictional creature. In each one of these cases, it made me feel like a fool. And so from the other side of the table he says, Matt, George Bush is the vice president of the United States and my heart soars because I know George Bush is not the vice president because I've never heard of George Bush and there's no way in the world that like I have not heard of the man who's vice president. So I stand up and I say no, it's Walter Mondale. And it's 1981, we don't have an internet to turn to and we have encyclopedias A through K only but they will not answer this question for us. So we go to what I have always thought of as the precursor to the internet, the device that we use to find ubiquitous information. Neil picks up the phone and he dials zero because in 1981 when you dial zero you can get an operator, a real human being and usually you ask a question like what's the phone number for Steve's pizza but in our house we use the operator to get facts. And they love it when you ask them because it's new and interesting and so he says, can you tell us who the vice president is and he holds the phone over the table

## [03:02 - 06:01]

and we listen as he says, George Bush. And Neil laughs at me and makes me feel stupid again and I just feel like I'm never going to beat this man. So two weeks later I'm sitting on the living room carpet and I'm watching our black and white TV, it's about nine o'clock, I'm watching Magnum PI when there's a knock on the door and Neil is in the office paying bills which enrages him and so he yells to me, answer the door. And so I go to the door and when I open it there's a stranger there, someone I don't recognize, a man. And his car is in the driveway and it's running and there's a woman and a boy in the car and they're looking up at my roof and the man says, I need to talk to your father. So I call Neil and I hear him stomping through the house and he gets to the door and the man says, you have a chimney fire, you need to call the fire department. And Neil scoffs at this man the same way he scoffs at me, he says, I'm burning green wood tonight, it's just sparking and he slams the door in the man's face and goes back to the bills. And so I wait for the commercial because I'm wondering like, what the hell is going on on my roof? So when the commercial comes I run outside to the road so I can see the house and I look up at the chimney, I don't know what a chimney fire is but my chimney has never done this. Like there are sparks and it's like a fireworks display coming out of the chimney so I run into the house, I go to the office and I say, Neil, I think we have a chimney fire. He's holding a pen that he's been writing the checks with and he slams it down so hard that everything on the desk shakes. And he says, God damn it, I'm burning green wood, go to bed. And I tell him like, I'm in the middle of Magnum PI, can I just finish it? And he says, no, which in 1981 is insanity because like, it'll be 25 years before I can Netflix this shit and find out like what happens to Magnum. Like it is insanity to end a show in the middle but he doesn't care, he says, go to bed. And I walk up those stairs just feeling like I am trapped with this man who I will never beat. In the middle of the night I'm awakened, someone is shaking me and when I open my eyes it's another stranger. And at first I'm terrified and then I look up and I see that he is wearing a firefighter's helmet. And without saying a word he picks me up like I weigh nothing, like I am a feather. And he starts moving me through my room and he says, don't worry, everything's fine. And as we pass into the hallway I see two other firefighters, they have an axe and they're hacking away at the wall to expose the chimney. And as we turn down the stairs I can see that my brother Jeremy and my sister Kelly, they're also being carried by firefighters. And I can hear Kelly coughing because the house is filled with smoke. And as we come into the kitchen I see Neil and my mom talking to a firefighter. And I just wait, I don't say a word. And finally Neil makes eye contact with me. And all I do is make a tiny verbal explosion. It is... Thank you.

## [06:02 - 06:42]

It's the greatest I told you so of my entire life. The firefighters will save our house that night. But six years later Neil will take my mother's disability money and disappear and we will lose that house. And I will not win the war against my stepfather. But on that night I win my first and only battle and it is a tiny victory. But as I leave that house and it burns behind me for the first time in a very long time I feel good about myself. Thank you. Thank you.
